roadmap.title: Cloud Security Engineer 6-Month Roadmap (180 Days)
roadmap.goal: Cloud Security Engineer → DevSecOps Engineer → Security Architect

target_profile.background: 3rd year IT student, Beginner-Intermediate Linux, Beginner Python, Basic networking
target_profile.resources: Mostly free (AWS Free Tier, TryHackMe, GitHub, YouTube)
target_profile.outcome: Job-ready junior cloud security engineer with GitHub portfolio

mini_project.title: Linux Command Reference Guide
mini_project.description: Create a GitHub repo documenting 50+ essential Linux commands with explanations, use cases, and examples

days.title: Linux History, Kernel, and Terminal Basics
learning_objectives: Understand Linux history and why it dominates cloud infrastructure
Grasp the difference between Linux kernel, distributions, and shells
youtube_search_terms: pwd ls cd chmod tutorial Linux

youtube_links.title: Linux Basics for Beginners - FreeCodeCamp
youtube_links.search_term: Linux basics tutorial for beginners FreeCodeCamp

documentation_links.title: The Linux Command Line - Full Book

hands_on_tasks: Open terminal and navigate to home directory using cd ~
List all files including hidden ones with ls -la
Navigate using absolute and relative paths
Use pwd to confirm current directory at each step
days.mini_challenge: Create a directory structure /home/user/cloud-sec/month1/week1/day1 using a single command chain. Then navigate through each directory and confirm path with pwd at each step.

github_task.title: Create Linux Basics Documentation
github_task.description: Document today's learning: 10 essential Linux commands with syntax, examples, and real-world use cases

days.title: File System Navigation & Listing Advanced Techniques
learning_objectives: Master ls command with all major flags
Understand file types and permissions at a glance
Learn to find and locate files efficiently
topics: File types: regular files, directories, symlinks, sockets
du and df for disk usage
youtube_search_terms: locate vs find Linux which faster
hands_on_tasks: Use ls -lh to list files with human-readable sizes
Sort files by modification time: ls -lt
Find all .txt files in home directory using find command
Use du -sh to check folder sizes
Practice find with multiple conditions: find ~ -name '*.log' -type f
days.mini_challenge: Find all files modified in the last 24 hours in your home directory. Then list them sorted by size, showing human-readable format.

github_task.title: File Finding Script & Documentation
github_task.description: Create a shell script that demonstrates 5 different find commands with output examples
deliverables: README explaining each command's use case

days.title: File Permissions & chmod Deep Dive
learning_objectives: Master Linux file permissions (rwx for user, group, other)
Know when and how to modify permissions securely
topics: Numeric vs symbolic notation: 755 vs u+rwx,g+rx,o+rx
youtube_search_terms: Linux setuid setgid sticky bit explained
hands_on_tasks: Set permissions using numeric: chmod 755 file1.txt
Set permissions using symbolic: chmod u+rwx,g+rx,o-rwx file2.txt
Create a script and make it executable: chmod +x script.sh
Change file owner: sudo chown user:group file.txt
days.mini_challenge: Create a directory with 3 files. Set permissions so only the owner can read/write/execute the directory, only the owner can read the files, and a script is executable. Verify with ls -l.

github_task.title: Permissions Lab & Documentation
github_task.description: Create comprehensive guide on Linux permissions with practical examples
deliverables: permissions-guide.md with all permission types explained

revision_topics: How permissions affect security (principle of least privilege)
Why execute permission matters on directories
When to use setuid and security implications

days.title: User & Group Management - Essential for Security
learning_objectives: Understand Linux user and group concepts
Assign users to groups for access control
hands_on_tasks: Check your current user: whoami && id
Create a new group: sudo groupadd security-team
days.mini_challenge: Find all users with UID >= 1000 (regular users) on your system. Create a script that lists them with their full names and home directories. Parse /etc/passwd to extract this info.

github_task.title: User Management Audit Script
github_task.description: Create Bash script to audit user accounts and security status
deliverables: user-audit.sh that finds users with UID 0, no password set, etc

revision_topics: Why /etc/shadow is readable only by root
Principle of least privilege and user segmentation

days.title: Process Management & System Monitoring
topics: Process tree: pstree and ps forest
top and htop for real-time monitoring
kill, killall, pkill for terminating processes
youtube_search_terms: Linux process management ps kill commands
hands_on_tasks: Show process tree: ps -ef or pstree
Filter processes by name: ps aux | grep bash
Start a process in background: sleep 1000 &
days.mini_challenge: Start 5 background processes (use sleep 3600). Then use ps to find them, create a process tree, and kill them using different methods (killall, pkill, kill -9).

github_task.title: Process Monitoring Script
github_task.description: Create Bash script for monitoring and managing system processes

revision_topics: Process zombie states and orphaned processes

days.title: System Logs & Troubleshooting Fundamentals
topics: tail, grep, awk for log analysis
youtube_search_terms: Linux log analysis grep awk tail
hands_on_tasks: Find failed SSH attempts: sudo grep 'Failed password' /var/log/auth.log
Count authentication attempts: sudo cat /var/log/auth.log | wc -l
days.mini_challenge: Find all failed SSH login attempts in auth.log from the past 24 hours. Count them, extract the attacking IP addresses, and create a simple report showing the most active attacker IP.

github_task.title: Security Log Analysis Script
github_task.description: Create Bash script for analyzing authentication and security logs
deliverables: log-analyzer.sh that parses auth.log for security events

revision_topics: How logs help with forensics and incident response

days.title: Week 1 Integration Lab & GitHub Project
learning_objectives: Integrate all Week 1 concepts into practical exercises
Build a comprehensive Linux audit script
topics: Integration of: ls, find, chmod, users, processes, logs
hands_on_tasks: Create comprehensive Linux audit script combining all Week 1 topics
Script should check: users with UID 0, world-writable files, failed logins, running processes
Add output examples to the repo
days.mini_challenge: Create a master Linux audit script (audit.sh) that integrates concepts from all 7 days: list users, check dangerous permissions, review recent logs, show running services. Output should be detailed and exportable as a security report.

github_task.title: Week 1 Capstone: Linux Security Audit Script
github_task.description: Comprehensive audit script combining all Week 1 learning
deliverables: USAGE.md - How to run and interpret results

mini_project.title: System Administration Helper Scripts Collection
mini_project.description: Suite of Bash scripts for common system administration and security monitoring tasks

days.title: Bash Scripting Fundamentals - Variables, Operators, Control Flow
learning_objectives: Master Bash variables and data types
topics: Variables: assignment, naming conventions, special variables ($0, $1, $#, $?, $$)
hands_on_tasks: Create script with variable assignment and echo
Write if statement checking file existence: if [ -f $file ]
Create for loop iterating over files
Write while loop for user input
Use case statement for multiple conditions
practice_commands: if [ -f file.txt ]; then echo 'File exists'; fi
for i in {1..10}; do echo $i; done
while read line; do echo $line; done < file.txt
days.mini_challenge: Create a Bash script that: (1) Takes a number as argument, (2) Checks if it's between 1-100, (3) If yes, loops and prints numbers, if no shows error message. Handle edge cases.

github_task.title: Bash Fundamentals Scripts
github_task.description: Collection of scripts demonstrating Bash variables, operators, and control flow

revision_topics: Bash special variables and their meanings
Test operators for files vs strings

days.title: Functions, Arrays, and Script Organization
hands_on_tasks: Create function that takes arguments and returns values
Implement array with for loop iteration
Create associative array for key-value storage
Add error handling with trap command
Implement logging function that timestamps messages
Create organized script with multiple functions
practice_commands: for item in ${arr[@]}; do echo $item; done
days.mini_challenge: Create reusable function for checking file/folder security permissions. Function should return 0 if secure, 1 if insecure. Use in loop through multiple directories. Include error handling and logging.

github_task.title: Advanced Bash Script with Functions
github_task.description: Production-quality script using functions, arrays, error handling

revision_topics: When to use associative arrays vs regular arrays
Error handling strategies in production scripts

days.title: Text Processing - grep, sed, awk Mastery
learning_objectives: Master text manipulation with grep, sed, awk
topics: grep: basic, extended regex, common flags
sed: stream editor, substitution, line manipulation
awk: field processing, pattern-action, built-in variables

youtube_links.title: grep, sed, awk Tutorials
youtube_links.search_term: Linux text processing grep sed awk

hands_on_tasks: Find lines in file matching pattern: grep 'pattern' file
Use regex with grep: grep -E '[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}' file
Substitute text with sed: sed 's/old/new/g' file
Extract specific fields with awk: awk -F: '{print $1,$3}' /etc/passwd
Filter lines with awk condition: awk '$3 > 1000' /etc/passwd
Combine tools: cat file | grep pattern | awk '{print $1}'
days.mini_challenge: Parse /etc/passwd file to: (1) Count users, (2) Find users with UID >= 1000, (3) Extract home directories, (4) Create report. Use combination of grep, awk, or sed.

github_task.title: Text Processing Utilities
github_task.description: Scripts demonstrating grep, sed, awk for security analysis

revision_topics: Regex special characters and when to escape
Performance: grep vs awk vs sed for large files

days.title: Piping, Redirection, and Advanced I/O Handling
learning_objectives: Master piping for efficient command chaining
youtube_search_terms: Linux file descriptors stdin stdout stderr
hands_on_tasks: Chain commands with pipe: cat file | grep pattern | wc -l
Redirect output to file: command > output.txt
Combine stdout and stderr: command &> all-output.log
Use tee to save and display: command | tee output.txt
days.mini_challenge: Create pipeline that: (1) Finds all .log files, (2) Filters for 'error' or 'warning', (3) Counts occurrences by file, (4) Saves report to file and displays top 10.

github_task.title: Advanced I/O and Piping Scripts
github_task.description: Demonstrate piping, redirection, and complex command chains

revision_topics: The importance of file descriptors in security
Performance implications of piping large datasets

days.title: Package Management and System Services
learning_objectives: Install, update, and remove software securely
hands_on_tasks: Update package list: sudo apt update
Install package: sudo apt install curl
Check installed version: apt-cache policy package
List installed packages: dpkg -l | grep pattern
Show service status: systemctl status ssh
Start/stop service: sudo systemctl start/stop ssh
Enable service on boot: sudo systemctl enable ssh
View service logs: sudo journalctl -u ssh.service
practice_commands: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade
days.mini_challenge: Install nginx webserver, enable it for auto-start, verify it's running, check its logs for the last 10 entries, and create a simple script that checks if it's running and restarts if needed.

github_task.title: Package Management Scripts
github_task.description: Scripts for managing packages and services

days.title: Networking Fundamentals in Bash - netstat, ss, iptables
learning_objectives: Monitor network traffic and listening ports
topics: netstat and ss for connection monitoring
DNS resolution tools: nslookup, dig, host
hands_on_tasks: Show all listening ports: sudo netstat -tlnp
List firewall rules: sudo ufw status
Add firewall rule: sudo ufw allow 22/tcp
days.mini_challenge: Create script that: (1) Lists all listening ports, (2) Identifies services on each port, (3) Checks if they're in approved list, (4) Generates security report of unexpected services.

github_task.title: Network Monitoring Scripts
github_task.description: Scripts for network diagnostics and security monitoring

revision_topics: TCP vs UDP and when each is used
How to detect unauthorized network activity

days.title: Week 2 Integration - Admin Scripts Collection
learning_objectives: Integrate all Week 2 concepts into production scripts
hands_on_tasks: Create script header with description, author, date, usage
Create configuration file for easy customization
Write detailed comments for complex logic
Commit to GitHub with meaningful messages
days.mini_challenge: Create comprehensive admin toolkit: security-audit.sh that combines weeks 1-2 concepts: user audit, permission check, process monitor, log analysis, network inspection. Must be production-quality with proper error handling and documentation.

github_task.title: Week 2 Capstone: Linux Admin Toolkit
github_task.description: Professional suite of administration and security scripts

revision_topics: Security implications of scripts running with sudo
Logging and audit trail best practices

mini_project.title: Network Calculator & Documentation Tool
mini_project.description: Interactive subnet calculator with network documentation generator

days.title: OSI Model & TCP/IP Fundamentals
learning_objectives: Grasp TCP/IP stack and how it relates to OSI
Know which protocols operate at each layer
topics: OSI Model: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application
TCP/IP Model: Link, Internet, Transport, Application
Protocols by layer: Ethernet, IP, TCP/UDP, HTTP/HTTPS
hands_on_tasks: Create OSI model diagram with examples
Map common protocols to OSI layers
Use Wireshark to observe packet encapsulation
Capture HTTP traffic and identify headers
Compare TCP vs UDP in traffic capture
days.mini_challenge: Capture network traffic for a web request. Analyze the capture to identify: (1) Which OSI layers are involved, (2) The PDUs at each layer, (3) The encapsulation/de-encapsulation process, (4) TCP handshake sequence.

github_task.title: OSI Model Documentation
github_task.description: Comprehensive guide to OSI model with examples

revision_topics: Why understanding OSI is critical for security
Where firewalls operate in the OSI model

days.title: IP Addressing and CIDR Notation
topics: IP address classes: A, B, C, D, E
hands_on_tasks: Convert netmask to CIDR and vice versa
Calculate network address for given IP and netmask
Practice binary conversion for IP addresses
Use calculator tools to verify calculations
days.mini_challenge: Given subnet 10.0.0.0/22: (1) Identify network address, broadcast, first/last usable host, (2) Create 4 subnets of equal size, (3) Plan IP allocation for VPC with 3 subnets (Public, Private App, Private DB).

github_task.title: Subnetting Reference & Calculator
github_task.description: Complete subnetting guide with Python calculator

revision_topics: Why CIDR is better than classful routing
Private vs public IP ranges and their uses

days.title: DNS, DHCP, and ARP Protocols
topics: DNS record types: A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, NS, SOA, TXT
DNS resolution process: recursive vs iterative
DHCP address allocation: DISCOVER, OFFER, REQUEST, ACK
hands_on_tasks: Query DNS with dig: dig example.com
Check DNS records: dig example.com MX
Monitor DHCP: sudo tail -f /var/log/syslog | grep DHCP
days.mini_challenge: Perform full DNS trace for a domain: (1) Query root nameservers, (2) Query TLD nameservers, (3) Query authoritative nameserver, (4) Extract all record types, (5) Create diagram showing resolution path.

github_task.title: DNS Troubleshooting Guide
github_task.description: DNS and DHCP diagnostics and troubleshooting

days.title: VPC, Network Interfaces, and Routing
learning_objectives: Know routing tables and gateway basics
topics: VPC: Virtual Private Cloud and isolation
hands_on_tasks: Create Internet Gateway and attach to VPC
Create NAT Gateway in public subnet
days.mini_challenge: Design and implement 3-tier VPC: Web tier (public, 10.0.1.0/24), App tier (private, 10.0.2.0/24), DB tier (private, 10.0.3.0/24). Configure routing and NAT. Document the architecture with diagram.

github_task.title: VPC Architecture Documentation
github_task.description: Complete VPC design with Terraform templates

days.title: Network Protocols: TCP Handshake & UDP
topics: TCP three-way handshake: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK
TCP flags: SYN, ACK, FIN, RST, PSH, URG
TCP connection states: LISTEN, ESTABLISHED, TIME_WAIT, CLOSE_WAIT
UDP vs TCP: When to use each
hands_on_tasks: Compare TCP vs UDP traffic patterns
Use tcpdump to capture and filter packets
days.mini_challenge: Capture network traffic for: (1) Successful TCP connection, (2) TCP connection reset, (3) UDP DNS query. Analyze each and create detailed diagram showing packet sequence with flags and sequence numbers.

github_task.title: TCP/UDP Protocol Analysis
github_task.description: Network packet analysis with Wireshark/tcpdump

revision_topics: TCP SYN floods and DDoS attacks
How firewalls detect malicious TCP behavior

days.title: Network Security: Firewalls, Ports, and Filtering
hands_on_tasks: View current iptables rules: sudo iptables -L -n
View UFW status: sudo ufw status
Allow HTTP/HTTPS: sudo ufw allow 80/tcp && sudo ufw allow 443/tcp
Deny specific port: sudo ufw deny 23/tcp
Test rules with nmap: nmap -p 1-1000 localhost
days.mini_challenge: Configure UFW firewall to: (1) Allow SSH from 10.0.0.0/8 only, (2) Allow HTTP/HTTPS from anywhere, (3) Deny all other traffic, (4) Enable logging, (5) Create report showing rules and denied packets.

github_task.title: Firewall Configuration Guide
github_task.description: UFW and iptables configuration for security

revision_topics: Principle of least privilege in firewall rules

days.title: Week 3 Integration - Network Design Project
hands_on_tasks: Design IP addressing scheme with security in mind
Define firewall rules for each tier
days.mini_challenge: Design complete secure network for startup: Web tier (scalable, public), App tier (internal only), Database tier (highly restricted). Include: VPC design, subnetting, routing, security groups, NAT, monitoring. Create professional documentation with diagrams.

github_task.title: Week 3 Capstone: Secure Network Design
github_task.description: Complete network architecture design with documentation

revision_topics: Review all networking concepts from Month 1

mini_project.title: Python Network Tools Suite
mini_project.description: Collection of Python scripts for network utilities and security tools

days.title: Python Fundamentals - Variables, Types, Operations
learning_objectives: Understand data types and type conversion
topics: Data types: int, float, str, bool, list, dict, tuple, set
List operations: indexing, slicing, append, extend
hands_on_tasks: Create and manipulate lists: lst = [1,2,3]; lst.append(4)
Use in/not in operators for membership
days.mini_challenge: Write Python script that: (1) Takes user input for IP address, (2) Validates format (basic check), (3) Splits into octets, (4) Performs arithmetic on octets, (5) Outputs results in formatted string.

github_task.title: Python Fundamentals Examples
github_task.description: Collection of scripts demonstrating Python basics

days.title: Python Control Flow - Conditionals and Loops

youtube_links.title: Python Control Flow
youtube_links.search_term: Python control flow if else loops

hands_on_tasks: Use for loop with range: for i in range(1, 11)
Use enumerate: for i, val in enumerate(list)
Use break to exit loop early
Create list comprehension: [x*2 for x in range(10)]
Create dictionary comprehension: {x: x*2 for x in range(5)}
practice_commands:
python3 << 'EOF'
for i in range(1, 6):
    print(i)
EOF
python3 -c "lst = [x*2 for x in range(10)]; print(lst)"
days.mini_challenge: Create script that: (1) Iterates through numbers 1-100, (2) Checks if each is prime, (3) Uses list comprehension to filter, (4) Prints primes and count.

github_task.title: Control Flow Examples
github_task.description: Conditional and loop patterns in Python

revision_topics: Control flow for parsing and filtering data

days.title: Python Functions and Modules
learning_objectives: Understand parameter passing and return values
topics: Standard library modules: os, sys, json, re
hands_on_tasks: Use return statement with multiple values
Import specific function: from os import path
practice_commands:
python3 << 'EOF'
def greet(name, greeting='Hello'):
    return f'{greeting}, {name}!'
print(greet('World'))
print(greet('World', 'Hi'))
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
def print_args(*args, **kwargs):
    print(args, kwargs)
print_args(1, 2, 3, x=10, y=20)
EOF
days.mini_challenge: Create module with functions: (1) validate_ip(ip) - checks IP format, (2) check_port_open(host, port) - basic check, (3) subnet_calculator(ip, prefix) - returns network info. Import and use in another script.

github_task.title: Python Modules and Functions
github_task.description: Reusable function library and module organization

days.title: Python File I/O and Error Handling
learning_objectives: Read and write files in Python
Work with JSON and CSV formats
topics: Opening and closing files: open(), with statement
Exception types: ValueError, TypeError, FileNotFoundError, IOError
hands_on_tasks: Read file: with open('file.txt') as f: data = f.read()
Write file: with open('output.txt', 'w') as f: f.write('data')
Read JSON: import json; data = json.load(open('file.json'))
Implement try/except for file not found
Parse CSV: import csv; reader = csv.reader(open('file.csv'))
practice_commands:
python3 << 'EOF'
with open('test.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('hello')
with open('test.txt') as f:
    print(f.read())
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import json
data = {'name': 'John', 'age': 25}
json.dump(data, open('data.json', 'w'))
data2 = json.load(open('data.json'))
print(data2)
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
try:
    with open('missing.txt') as f:
        f.read()
except FileNotFoundError:
    print('File not found')
EOF
days.mini_challenge: Create script that: (1) Reads configuration from JSON file, (2) Reads security audit data from CSV, (3) Processes and combines data, (4) Generates report JSON, (5) Handles errors gracefully.

github_task.title: File I/O and Data Processing
github_task.description: File handling and error management scripts

days.title: Git Workflow and GitHub Collaboration
topics: Git basics: init, add, commit, status
hands_on_tasks: Push to remote: git push origin feature/new-feature
Merge to main: git merge feature/new-feature
days.mini_challenge: Create GitHub repository with: (1) Main branch with README, (2) Develop branch, (3) Feature branch for new function, (4) Create and merge pull request, (5) Add proper .gitignore, (6) Tag version 1.0.0.

github_task.title: Git Workflow Demonstration
github_task.description: Complete Git workflow with proper branching

revision_topics: Git workflows: Git Flow, GitHub Flow

days.title: Python Practical Project - Network Tool
hands_on_tasks: Write main script with argparse for arguments
Commit to GitHub with proper structure
days.mini_challenge: Create network audit tool (subnet-checker.py): (1) Takes network range, (2) Validates it, (3) Generates subnets, (4) Calculates host counts, (5) Exports to JSON/CSV, (6) Proper error handling, (7) Logging, (8) GitHub ready.

github_task.title: Month 1 Capstone: Python Network Tool
github_task.description: Production-quality Python project

days.title: Month 1 Review & Security Fundamentals Primer
learning_objectives: Review all Month 1 concepts: Linux, Bash, Networking, Python, Git
Understand CIA triad and security fundamentals
Prepare for cloud security in Month 2
hands_on_tasks: Review and polish all GitHub repositories from Month 1
Create Month 1 summary document with key takeaways
Run all scripts created during the month and fix any issues
Write security analysis applying CIA triad to your Linux audit script
Create a self-assessment checklist for Month 1 skills
Set up AWS account for Month 2 preparation
practice_commands: Run shellcheck on all Bash scripts
python3 -m py_compile script.py for all Python files
days.mini_challenge: Create comprehensive Month 1 portfolio README: list all projects, skills learned, commands mastered, and security concepts understood. Include links to all GitHub repos and a self-assessment rating for each skill area.

github_task.title: Month 1 Portfolio & Review
github_task.description: Comprehensive Month 1 review and portfolio consolidation

weeks.theme: AWS Account Setup, Shared Responsibility Model, IAM Basics

mini_project.title: IAM Policy Analyzer Tool
mini_project.description: Python tool for analyzing and validating AWS IAM policies

days.title: AWS Account Setup and Best Practices
topics: AWS Free Tier details and limits
days.mini_challenge: Set up AWS account: (1) Enable MFA, (2) Set billing alert for $10, (3) Enable CloudTrail, (4) Create cost anomaly detection, (5) Document account setup process.

github_task.title: AWS Account Setup Documentation
github_task.description: Complete account setup guide and security checklist

days.title: Shared Responsibility Model and AWS Security Foundation
learning_objectives: Know what AWS vs you are responsible for
Build security from the ground up
topics: AWS responsibility: infrastructure, physical, network, hypervisor
Customer responsibility: OS, application, network config, IAM, encryption
Encryption: in transit vs at rest
hands_on_tasks: Create responsibility matrix for common services
Create security groups with minimal permissions
days.mini_challenge: Create security matrix: (1) List common AWS services (EC2, S3, RDS), (2) Define responsibilities for each, (3) Document security controls needed, (4) Create implementation checklist.

github_task.title: Security Responsibility Model
github_task.description: Detailed shared responsibility documentation

revision_topics: How responsibility shifts with managed services
Security automation and infrastructure as code

days.title: IAM Fundamentals - Users, Groups, Roles, and Policies
learning_objectives: Master IAM basics for identity and access
Create and manage users, groups, and roles
Implement least privilege from the start
topics: Roles: For services and temporary access
hands_on_tasks: Create custom policy for specific permissions
Test policy with IAM Policy Simulator
Set up access keys for CLI
days.mini_challenge: Create IAM setup: (1) Create developers group with EC2 read-only policy, (2) Create EC2-role for instances, (3) Create user and add to group, (4) Generate access keys, (5) Test permissions with simulator.

github_task.title: IAM Architecture and Policies
github_task.description: Complete IAM setup guide with policy examples

days.title: IAM Policy Deep Dive and Permission Evaluation
learning_objectives: Master IAM policy language and syntax
Create principle of least privilege policies
topics: Policy structure: Version, Statement, Effect, Principal, Action, Resource, Condition
Conditions: date, IP address, SSL/TLS, etc
Policy evaluation logic: explicit deny, explicit allow, implicit deny

youtube_links.title: IAM Policy Deep Dive
youtube_links.search_term: AWS IAM policy evaluation deep dive

hands_on_tasks: Write policy to allow EC2 read access only
Write policy with conditional IP restrictions
Write policy for specific S3 bucket operations
Create policy for CloudWatch logs access
practice_commands: aws iam test-custom-policy --policy-input-list file://policy.json --action-names ec2:DescribeInstances --resource-arns arn:aws:ec2:*:*:instance/*
days.mini_challenge: Create restrictive policy: (1) Allow EC2 describe operations, (2) Restrict to only prod instances with tags, (3) Require MFA for termination, (4) Allow CloudWatch logs for debugging, (5) Test in simulator.

github_task.title: Advanced IAM Policies
github_task.description: Complex policy scenarios and conditional access

revision_topics: Common IAM mistakes and how to avoid them
Auditing IAM permissions with IAM Access Analyzer

days.title: IAM Security Best Practices and MFA
hands_on_tasks: Review CloudTrail logs for IAM activity
Set up AWS Config for compliance
days.mini_challenge: Implement IAM security: (1) Enable MFA for all users, (2) Set strong password policy, (3) Rotate access keys, (4) Enable CloudTrail, (5) Run IAM Access Analyzer, (6) Document findings.

github_task.title: IAM Security Implementation
github_task.description: Complete security hardening guide

days.title: IAM Policy Analysis Tool Project
hands_on_tasks: Write Python script to download policies
days.mini_challenge: Create IAM Policy Analyzer: (1) Download user/role policies, (2) Analyze for security issues, (3) Check for overly permissive (*), (4) Generate recommendations, (5) Export report as JSON/HTML.

github_task.title: IAM Policy Analyzer Tool
github_task.description: Python tool for IAM policy security analysis

days.title: Week 5 Integration - AWS CLI Automation & IAM Review
learning_objectives: Integrate all IAM and AWS account concepts
Automate IAM tasks with AWS CLI
topics: AWS CLI automation for IAM tasks
Scripting IAM user and role management
hands_on_tasks: Create Bash script to automate IAM user creation with proper groups
Script to audit all IAM users and their last access
Automate access key rotation with AWS CLI
Create security baseline document for new AWS accounts
Push all Week 5 work to GitHub
practice_commands: aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d
days.mini_challenge: Create AWS IAM security audit script: (1) List all users and their MFA status, (2) Find unused access keys older than 90 days, (3) Check for overly permissive policies, (4) Generate compliance report as JSON.

github_task.title: Week 5 Capstone: IAM Security Automation
github_task.description: Complete IAM automation and security baseline project

mini_project.title: EC2 Security Hardening Suite

days.title: Launching, Connecting, and Managing EC2 Instances
topics: Instance user data and initialization scripts
Systems Manager Session Manager as alternative to SSH
youtube_search_terms: AWS EC2 launch and connect tutorial
hands_on_tasks: Create security group with SSH (port 22) from your IP
Download and use key to SSH into instance
practice_commands: aws ec2 create-key-pair --key-name my-key --query 'KeyMaterial' --output text > my-key.pem
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id sg-xxx --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr YOUR_IP/32
aws ec2 run-instances --image-id ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0 --instance-type t2.micro --key-name my-key
days.mini_challenge: Launch EC2 instance: (1) Create key pair securely, (2) Create security group with SSH access, (3) Launch instance, (4) SSH into it and verify OS, (5) Check metadata service, (6) Stop and terminate properly.

Add SSH rule from specific IP
Create security group for database (MySQL port 3306)
practice_commands: aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id sg-xxx --protocol tcp --port 3306 --source-group sg-yyy
aws ec2 create-network-acl-entry --network-acl-id nacl-xxx --rule-number 100 --protocol tcp --port-range FromPort=80,ToPort=80 --cidr-block 0.0.0.0/0 --ingress
